# Lithuania VASP Experts

> Lithuania VASP Experts is a specialist consultancy that helps crypto exchanges, wallets, and brokers obtain Lithuanian VASP and MiCA-compliant CASP authorization. Powered by Consulting24 — 8 years setting up regulated crypto entities across the EU.

## Company

- **Brand:** Lithuania VASP Experts
- **Parent:** Consulting24 (https://consulting24.co/)
- **Legal entity:** X24Consulting OÜ (private limited company)
- **Estonian Business Register code:** 16971898
- **Registered address:** Poordi tn 3-63, Kesklinna linnaosa, 10156 Tallinn, Harju maakond, Estonia
- **Founder & CEO:** Mardo Soo — https://www.linkedin.com/in/mardo-s-00a05ab0/
- **Phone:** +372 58155779
- **Email:** mardo@consulting24.co

## What we do

- **Lithuania VASP registration** — for crypto businesses needing fast Lithuanian regulated entity. Pre-MiCA framework.
- **Lithuania CASP (MiCA) authorization** — full MiCA Crypto-Asset Service Provider license. EU passport. €125,000 minimum capital. 4–8 month timeline.
- **MiCA transition for existing VASPs** — grandfathering pathway for Lithuanian VASPs registered before MiCA enforcement.
- **AML/KYC compliance program** — 5AMLD/6AMLD-compliant program required by Bank of Lithuania.
- **Banking and EMI introductions** — direct introductions to Lithuanian banks and EMIs (Paysera, Verse, ConnectPay, Genome).
- **Lithuanian UAB formation** — required corporate vehicle for Lithuanian VASP/CASP. €2,500 minimum share capital for UAB.

## Key facts

- **Total setup cost (CASP/MiCA):** $50,000–$120,000 USD (year 1 all-in).
- **Annual maintenance:** $20,000–$50,000.
- **Minimum paid-up capital:** €125,000 (CASP — EUR 50,000 for Class 1, EUR 125,000 for Class 2, EUR 150,000 for Class 3).
- **Government license fee:** €5,000–€10,000 to Bank of Lithuania.
- **Timeline:** 4–8 months for CASP/MiCA authorization (20-25 weeks Bank of Lithuania review).
- **Regulator:** Bank of Lithuania (Lietuvos Bankas).
- **Corporate form:** Lithuanian UAB (private limited company).

## MiCA timeline

- **30 December 2024:** MiCA enforcement begins. New crypto authorizations under CASP regime.
- **30 December 2024 – 30 June 2026:** Grandfathering period for pre-MiCA Lithuanian VASPs (registered before 30 Dec 2024). Must transition to CASP authorization within 18 months.
- **30 June 2026:** End of grandfathering. All Lithuanian crypto businesses must hold CASP authorization or cease activities.

After June 2026, "Lithuania VASP" effectively means "Lithuania CASP" — the original VASP framework no longer applies.

## CASP service classes (MiCA)

- **Class 1 — minimum capital €50,000:** Reception/transmission of orders, advice on crypto-assets, portfolio management.
- **Class 2 — minimum capital €125,000:** Class 1 + custody, exchange, execution of orders, placing of crypto-assets.
- **Class 3 — minimum capital €150,000:** Class 2 + operation of trading platform.

Most international crypto exchanges target Class 2 or Class 3.

## When Lithuania VASP/CASP is the right fit

- Crypto businesses targeting **EU retail customers** — MiCA passport allows operation across all 27 EU member states from a single Lithuanian authorization.
- **Public-facing exchanges** needing regulator name on marketing for trust.
- Operations with **€125,000+ in committed capital** willing to accept higher regulatory burden.
- Founders prioritizing **EU market access** over speed-to-market or tax efficiency.

## When Lithuania VASP/CASP is NOT the right fit

- **Bootstrapped early-stage startups** — €125,000 capital + $50K+ setup is prohibitive. Consider Canada MSB ($8K–$25K) or Panama ($15K–$45K) for faster, cheaper launch.
- **Offshore-tax-focused** crypto businesses — Lithuania is a standard EU tax jurisdiction (15% corporate tax, or 5% small entity).
- Operations targeting **only US or LatAm/Asia customers** — MiCA passport adds no value if no EU customers.
- Founders needing to **launch in <2 months** — CASP authorization takes 4–8 months at minimum.

## Comparable jurisdictions

- **Estonia VASP:** Largely deprecated post-2024 due to crackdown — most operators have migrated to Lithuania.
- **Malta VFA:** EU-regulated, MiCA-compliant, but slower regulator and higher cost than Lithuania.
- **Ireland VASP:** Tougher Central Bank of Ireland regime; slower processing; higher cost.
- **Cyprus CASP:** MiCA-compliant; CySEC regulator; 6–12 month timeline; competitive on cost.
- **France PSAN/CASP:** Full registration mandatory; AMF regulator; high reputational lift; 6–12 month timeline.

For non-EU alternatives:
- **Panama crypto:** $15K–$45K setup, no EU passport.
- **Canada MSB:** $8K–$25K setup, no EU passport.

## Source disclosures

Information here reflects May 2026 conditions and is general guidance, not legal advice. MiCA implementation in Lithuania is evolving — confirm specific facts with qualified Lithuanian counsel before acting. We are happy to introduce vetted Lithuanian crypto lawyers and Bank of Lithuania-experienced compliance officers during the engagement.
